overview: FAME India Scheme (Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Hybrid and Electric Vehicles) is a Government of India initiative launched in 2015 to promote the adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles through financial incentives and support for charging infrastructure. The scheme is implemented in phases, with FAME-I operational from 2015 to 2019 and FAME-II launched in April 2019 with a total outlay of ₹10,000 crores for a period of three years, extendable. It aims to accelerate the uptake of environmentally friendly vehicles by providing upfront incentives to buyers and support for charging infrastructure development.
target_beneficiaries: buyers of electric and hybrid vehicles
objectives: Promote faster adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles in India
Reduce dependence on fossil fuels and address vehicular emissions
Support the development of charging infrastructure across the country
Encourage indigenous manufacturing of electric vehicles and components
Create demand for electric vehicles through upfront incentives
Establish a sustainable ecosystem for electric mobility
eligibility: Eligibility varies by vehicle segment and buyer type. For individual buyers, eligibility is based on the purchase of approved electric two-wheelers, three-wheelers, four-wheelers (including passenger cars and light commercial vehicles), and buses that meet specified technical standards and are listed on the FAME portal. Manufacturers must have their models tested and approved by designated agencies (like ARAI) and registered on the FAME portal. Institutional buyers and fleet operators can also avail incentives for qualifying vehicles used for public transport, shared mobility, or commercial purposes.
benefits: Financial incentives are provided at the point of sale to reduce the upfront cost of electric vehicles. The incentive amount varies by vehicle category: up to ₹15,000 per kWh for two-wheelers (capped at 40% of vehicle cost), up to ₹10,000 per kWh for three-wheelers, up to ₹10,000 per kWh for four-wheelers (capped at ₹1.5 lakh for cars and ₹1 lakh for LCVs), and up to ₹20,000 per kWh for buses. Additionally, the scheme supports the installation of charging infrastructure through subsidies for setting up public, semi-public, and private charging stations.
financial_support: Under FAME-II, a total financial outlay of ₹10,000 crores has been allocated for a period of three years (extendable). Of this, ₹8,596 crores is allocated for demand incentives to support the purchase of electric vehicles, ₹1,000 crores for charging infrastructure, ₹200 crores for administrative expenses, and ₹204 crores for publicity, monitoring, and evaluation. The incentives are transferred directly to the manufacturers or sellers, who then pass on the benefit to the end consumers by reducing the vehicle's purchase price.
grant_amount_per_entity: Incentive varies by vehicle type: up to ₹15,000/kWh for two-wheelers (max 40% of cost), ₹10,000/kWh for three- and four-wheelers, and ₹20,000/kWh for buses, with specific caps per vehicle category (e.g., ₹1.5 lakh for electric cars, ₹1 lakh for LCVs, and proportionally higher for buses based on battery size).
application_process: 1. Manufacturers must get their electric vehicle models tested and approved by designated testing agencies (e.g., ARAI, ICAT). 2. Approved models are registered on the FAME portal by the manufacturer. 3. Buyers purchase the eligible vehicle from a registered dealer. 4. The dealer or manufacturer uploads the sale details on the FAME portal. 5. The Department of Heavy Industries verifies the claim and disburses the incentive amount to the manufacturer/dealer. 6. The end consumer benefits from the reduced purchase price at the point of sale.
required_documents: Vehicle invoice showing chassis number and model details
FAME compliance certificate from the manufacturer
Bank account details of the beneficiary (for direct benefit transfer, if applicable)
Aadhaar card of the buyer (for individual beneficiaries)
deadlines: FAME-II was initially launched for a period of three years from April 1, 2019, and has been extended multiple times; currently operational until further notice as per government notifications.
implementing_agency: Department of Heavy Industries, Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises
caveats: Incentives are available only for vehicles models approved and registered on the FAME portal
The benefit is passed on to the consumer only if the vehicle is purchased from a registered dealer
Funds are subject to availability and government approvals; the scheme may be modified or withdrawn based on budgetary allocations
Charging infrastructure support is subject to technical guidelines and site approval by concerned authorities
source_cited_notes: https://fame.gov.in/ — Main portal providing scheme details, vehicle list, and operational guidelines
